analyzed_capabilities: Repository code edits and test creation
New runtime scaffolding for uncertified governance capabilities
Local pytest and governance conformance command execution
Manual interpretation of hook conformance drift and repository release posture

provider_source_evidence.evidence_summary: OpenAI API docs expose code generation, structured output, function calling, tool use, Agents SDK, shell, apply-patch, and local shell tool surfaces.
Claude API docs expose tool-use infrastructure including programmatic tool calling, code execution, bash, text editor, and computer-use tool categories.
Claude Code is documented as an agentic coding tool that can read codebases, edit files, run commands, and work from terminal and IDE surfaces.
Mistral function calling allows models to connect to external local tools, with developer-defined functions or APIs and developer-executed tool results.
Mistral model catalog includes open-weight general, coding, and code-agent models including Mistral Large 3 Open, Mistral Small 4 Open, and Devstral 2 Open.

reclassified_capabilities.capability: Repository code edits and test creation
reclassified_capabilities.rationale: The capability needs governed diff synthesis, filesystem mutation, and test creation. Codex is one execution surface, but the core need is a governed coding model plus a mutation-capable worker under human approval.
governance_constraints: Human approval remains mandatory before repository mutation.
Replay lineage and diff evidence must be preserved.
Fail-closed validation must block unapproved changes.

reclassified_capabilities.capability: New runtime scaffolding for uncertified governance capabilities
reclassified_capabilities.rationale: Runtime scaffolding requires design synthesis and code generation, then governed file creation. The cognition component can be provider-neutral if output is validated before mutation.
governance_constraints: Generated runtime scope must be bounded by implementation request artifacts.
Tests and replay evidence remain mandatory.
Human authority remains final for acceptance.

reclassified_capabilities.capability: Local pytest and governance conformance command execution
reclassified_capabilities.rationale: The capability is command execution plus result capture. It does not require Codex cognition if a governed local worker can run allowlisted commands and preserve logs.
governance_constraints: Commands must be allowlisted or explicitly approved.
No hidden repair or mutation may be triggered by validation failure.

reclassified_capabilities.capability: Manual interpretation of hook conformance drift and repository release posture
reclassified_capabilities.rationale: The final decision is constitutional and operational, so human authority is required. A generic cognition provider can summarize evidence, but cannot replace final authority.
governance_constraints: Human release authority cannot be delegated.

limitations: API model can propose code and tool calls, but repository mutation still requires a governed worker.
Human approval and replay capture must be externalized through AiGOL.
API tool use requires developer-provided tools and result handling.
Governed mutation must be supplied by AiGOL worker controls.
Still needs AiGOL governance wrappers for approval, replay, certification, and fail-closed boundaries.
Provider substitution does not remove human authority requirements.
Function calling connects to external local tools but the developer or runtime executes the tools.
Repository mutation and command execution require a separate governed worker.
Local model quality and context capacity must be certified against Sapianta runtime tasks.
A governed local mutation worker remains required.
Future local models require capability certification before production use.
Provider independence does not eliminate execution governance.

interpretation.summary: The remaining dependency is not Codex-specific. All four previously Codex-required capabilities can be reframed as generic cognition, generic execution worker, and human authority requirements.
interpretation.blocking_condition: AiGOL still needs a certified generic execution worker for repository mutation and allowlisted command execution before Codex can be removed from implementation execution.

non_goals: Do not claim provider substitution authorizes autonomous repository mutation.
Do not remove human approval gates.
Do not treat model output as certification evidence without AiGOL replay validation.
Do not hide that local model feasibility requires task-specific certification.
